description_long: This is a portable metal crowd control barrier, commonly used in urban environments to manage pedestrian flow or restrict access to certain areas. The structure consists of a rectangular frame with evenly spaced vertical bars, all painted in a bright yellow color for high visibility. The barrier stands on two curved feet, each with a flat base for stability, and features small hooks or latches on the sides for interlocking with adjacent barriers. The metal construction suggests durability and resistance to outdoor conditions, while the open design allows for visibility through the barrier. The ends of the frame are rounded, minimizing sharp edges and enhancing safety. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and ease of deployment in public spaces.
description: A yellow metal crowd control barrier with vertical bars and curved feet, designed for high visibility and easy interlocking. Its open, sturdy frame is ideal for managing pedestrian flow in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the barrier, vertical bars, and both curved feet.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profile and the curvature of the feet, with hooks visible on the side.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the vertical bars and feet from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, highlighting the slim profile and side hooks.
